jokes.text: $2.50 for this? What a gyp!
jokes.setup: Watching a McBain movie in theater
jokes.punchline: Immediate complaint about ticket price for low-quality movie
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about movie theater experience, economical but predictable

jokes.text: You broke up that meeting. Now I'm thinking about holding another meeting in bed.
jokes.setup: Action movie dialogue about breaking up a criminal meeting
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo using 'meeting' euphemistically
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts action movie expectations with sexual humor

jokes.text: The screen's small! The floor's sticky! The subplot felt tacked on. We demand a refund!
jokes.setup: Typical movie theater complaints
jokes.punchline: Escalates from physical complaints to sophisticated film criticism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane to pretentious complaints, with good structural build

jokes.text: Don't have a heart attack, old dude. Don't you tell me what to do, you young whipper...snapper!
jokes.setup: Young person telling old man not to have heart attack
jokes.punchline: Grampa gets so worked up he literally can't finish his old-fashioned insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Grampa's anger making the heart attack advice prophetic while he struggles with old-timey language

jokes.text: Thanks for nuclear power, which is yet to cause a single proven fatality...at least in this country. Amen.
jokes.setup: Family dinner prayer giving thanks
jokes.punchline: Thanks for nuclear power with increasingly specific qualifiers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of prayer format with dark nuclear humor and lawyer-like qualifiers, perfectly Homer

jokes.text: Bart ate during the blessing! You opened your eyes. Eating is worse. Is not! Is too!
jokes.setup: Catching each other breaking prayer etiquette
jokes.punchline: Debating whether eating or looking is worse sin during prayer
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamics and absurd moral hierarchy debate typical of kids

jokes.text: One more word and Bart gets no cartoons...and Lisa can't go to college! Dad! Not one word!
jokes.setup: Father threatening punishment for talking
jokes.punchline: Punishment escalates from no cartoons to no college, then enforced immediately
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's poor sense of proportional punishment

jokes.text: No panto-ma-mine, either.
jokes.setup: Forbidding the kids from talking
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'pantomime' while trying to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - trying to sound smart while butchering pronunciation

jokes.text: Not that close!
jokes.setup: Asks Homer to pull chair closer
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains when Homer complies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - contradictory and fussy, economically delivered

jokes.text: Oh, Dad! You and your imagination.
jokes.setup: Grampa says he'll die someday
jokes.punchline: Homer treats mortality as Grampa's silly fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to basic reality, treating death as whimsical notion

jokes.text: Hey, handsome, wanna dunk the clown?
jokes.setup: Grampa describing meeting Homer's mother at carnival
jokes.punchline: Innocent carnival game offer with sexual undertones given context
jokes.explanation: Double entendre working on innocent and sexual levels in flashback context

jokes.text: She did things your mother would never do. Like have sex for money.
jokes.setup: Building up mysterious things carnival woman did
jokes.punchline: Blunt revelation that she was a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and blunt delivery subverts the romantic setup

jokes.text: This makes me feel special. Since I'm the one you kept, it means you really love me.
jokes.setup: Learning about half-brother given up for adoption
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic that abandoning one child proves love for the other
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding positive spin on family trauma through flawed reasoning

jokes.text: Interesting theory.
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional declaration about being loved
jokes.punchline: Grampa's dry, noncommittal response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response deflates Homer's emotional moment

jokes.text: Yeah, right. Good luck.
jokes.setup: Homer vows to move heaven and earth to find his brother
jokes.punchline: Grampa's dismissive, sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response, in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: A long-lost half-brother. How Dickensian!
jokes.setup: Family discussing the half-brother situation
jokes.punchline: Lisa's precocious literary reference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lisa's intellectual pretensions with accurate literary reference

jokes.text: Know where this bastard lives? His parents aren't married. It's the correct word, isn't it? He's got us there.
jokes.setup: Bart using the word 'bastard'
jokes.punchline: Technical correctness of the term defeats parental objection
jokes.explanation: Clever use of literal meaning to circumvent censorship, good family dynamic

jokes.text: You're a little late. They tore that down 30 years ago. Thirty years! I'll never find him!
jokes.setup: Looking for the orphanage
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate despair when timeline seems long
jokes.explanation: Homer's immediate defeatism at minor obstacle, good character moment

jokes.text: Oh, brother, where art thou?
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his lost brother
jokes.punchline: Biblical/literary reference (inadvertently) appropriate to situation
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference that works both literally and as episode title, though Homer wouldn't get the allusion

jokes.text: Yeah, but we're looking for my brother today.
jokes.setup: Man mentions searching for his own lost twin brother
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses his parallel struggle with selfish focus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-centeredness dismissing someone else's identical problem

jokes.text: Your brother could be anywhere, even Detroit.
jokes.setup: Giving unhelpful general statement about brother's location
jokes.punchline: Detroit mentioned as example of 'anywhere' with negative implication
jokes.explanation: Mild dig at Detroit as undesirable location, setup for later revelation

jokes.text: If you ask me...the city of brotherly love isn't Philadelphia, it's Detroit.
jokes.setup: Previous mention of Detroit negatively
jokes.punchline: Clerk awkwardly trying to give hint about brother's location
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt at hinting that works as character moment for helpful clerk

jokes.text: Read between the lines, you fool!
jokes.setup: Dropping subtle hints about Detroit
jokes.punchline: Clerk's frustration at Homer's obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of clerk's frustration with Homer's density

jokes.text: Here's 20 bucks. Now, will you tell me where my brother lives? I don't-- Take it and tell me! He lives in Detroit.
jokes.setup: Clerk trying to maintain rules about confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude bribery immediately works
jokes.explanation: Simple corruption joke, shows Homer's direct approach to problems

jokes.text: 'H'! Of course! That could stand for Herbert!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to find Herbert Powell in phone book
jokes.punchline: Obvious realization delivered as brilliant insight
jokes.explanation: Homer treating obvious connection as eureka moment, good character stupidity

jokes.text: Hello? Hello? Stupid phone! Knock it off! I'm here! I was silent due to emotion.
jokes.setup: Phone conversation with apparent connection problem
jokes.punchline: Herb was just emotionally overwhelmed, not technical issue
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about phone problems, nice emotional moment undercut by explanation

jokes.text: But, Marge, I want to see my brother! Homer, it's an empty threat.
jokes.setup: Homer whining about turning around and going home
jokes.punchline: Marge matter-of-factly explains parental bluffing
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on parenting tactics, Marge's practical honesty

jokes.text: Sorry, sir, I didn't know it was you. That's okay. Carry on. See the way I handled that, Marge?
jokes.setup: Guard stopping car at entrance
jokes.punchline: Herb's mild response treated as masterful leadership display
jokes.explanation: Herb's slight ego showing through treating routine interaction as leadership example

jokes.text: We're losing to the Japanese. Why? Unfair trade practices? Mushy one-worlders in Washington? Some gypsy curse?
jokes.setup: Business meeting about losing market share to Japan
jokes.punchline: Escalates from legitimate to absurd explanations including gypsy curse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from reasonable to supernatural explanations for business problems

jokes.text: Why did I hire Harvard idiots? You went there. But Mommy and Daddy didn't pay my way!
jokes.setup: Herb complaining about Harvard-educated employees
jokes.punchline: Herb reveals his own Harvard education but claims different circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy and class resentment despite similar background

jokes.text: Persephone? What the hell kind of name is that? She was the Greek goddess of spring and rebirth. She was carried off to Hades where she ate six pomegranates-- People don't want cars named after hungry, old, Greek broads!
jokes.setup: Car name 'Persephone' being explained with mythology
jokes.punchline: Herb reduces complex mythology to 'hungry, old, Greek broads'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reduction of classical mythology to crude business terms, perfectly captures character

jokes.text: I guess they extend to when the Angles met the Saxons. When white met bread.
jokes.setup: Question about personal roots and background
jokes.punchline: Historical reference reduced to bread pun about whiteness/blandness
jokes.explanation: Clever pun connecting Anglo-Saxon history with bread/blandness metaphor

jokes.text: Holy moly! The bastard's rich!
jokes.setup: Seeing Herb's mansion
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude amazement using the 'bastard' term again
jokes.explanation: Good callback to bastard terminology with Homer's typical crudeness

jokes.text: All born in wedlock? Yes, but Bart was a close call.
jokes.setup: Herb asking about children's legitimacy
jokes.punchline: Marge's honest admission about Bart's conception timing
jokes.explanation: Marge's characteristic honesty revealing family history awkwardly

jokes.text: Lisa, are you the hell-raiser your father told me about? No, I can assure you I'm not. I'm the hell-raiser.
jokes.setup: Herb asking Lisa if she's the troublemaker
jokes.punchline: Lisa politely corrects while Bart claims the title
jokes.explanation: Good character distinction between Lisa's politeness and Bart's pride in troublemaking

jokes.text: Oh, what's to know? Just dive in. Catch!
jokes.setup: Herb nervous about holding baby
jokes.punchline: Homer literally throws the baby to him
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting approach - casual about baby safety, physical comedy implied

jokes.text: God, that new-baby smell. Homer, you're the richest man I know. I feel the same about you.
jokes.setup: Herb calling Homer richest man despite being wealthy himself
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the emotional meaning, thinks it's literal
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Herb's emotional metaphor and Homer's literal interpretation

jokes.text: Wait! Let me see if I got this right. It's Christmas day, 4:00 a.m...there's a rumble in my belly-- Homer!
jokes.setup: Homer fixated on pork chop availability
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hypothetical scenario about middle-of-night pork chop craving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obsession with food logistics, building elaborate fantasy scenario

jokes.text: 'Uncle Herb' sounds so formal. Do you think you could call me 'Unky Herb'? No problemo, Unky Herb.
jokes.setup: Herb wanting less formal family relationship
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at casual uncle nickname
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from Herb trying too hard to be casual family member

jokes.text: A millionaire! I kept the wrong one.
jokes.setup: Learning about Herb's wealth
jokes.punchline: Grampa expressing regret about which son he kept
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental favoritism based on financial success, perfectly Grampa

jokes.text: Got him!
jokes.setup: Homer spitting over yacht side
jokes.punchline: Homer's satisfaction at hitting someone below
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor, in character for Homer but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You know what they cost me? There's maybe $40 worth of steel in them.
jokes.setup: Homer amazed that Herb would give him a car
jokes.punchline: Herb reveals the actual minimal cost of manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Good business insider knowledge revealing markup realities

jokes.text: You hear that, morons? This is why we're getting killed! Instead of listening, you're telling people what they want.
jokes.setup: Homer representing American consumer desires
jokes.punchline: Herb's business insight about customer vs. corporate thinking
jokes.explanation: Good business satire about corporate disconnection from customers

jokes.text: I want to pay you $200,000 a year! And I want to let you!
jokes.setup: Herb offering Homer huge salary
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic acceptance as if he's doing Herb a favor
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to how job offers work, treating acceptance as generous favor

jokes.text: Your brother told you to help us. Yeah, he did. Why don't you get us some coffee?
jokes.setup: Engineers asking Homer for help with car design
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately delegates to them like a servant
jokes.explanation: Homer misunderstanding power dynamics, treating experts as servants

jokes.text: How could you ask for it? You don't know what it is. May I be excused?
jokes.setup: Homer asking for car features he doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike request to leave when confronted
jokes.explanation: Homer reverting to child behavior when challenged, good character regression

jokes.text: It's because you're an average schmo. All you need is self-confidence.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering why he got the job
jokes.punchline: Herb's backhanded compliment about being average
jokes.explanation: Good business logic turned into insult, Herb's honest assessment of Homer

jokes.text: Hello, Einstein! I said a place to put my drink. The Super Slurpees they sell at Kwik-E-Marts are this big!
jokes.setup: Engineer mentions car has beverage holder
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic dismissal and specific size requirements
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical demands conflicting with standard car design, consumer insight

jokes.text: Put a horn here, here and here. I can't find it when I'm mad. And they should play 'La Cucaracha.'
jokes.setup: Homer designing car features
jokes.punchline: Multiple horns for anger issues playing Mexican song
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's road rage solutions with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: You're fired! What are we paying you for? A separate soundproof dome for the kids? With restraints and muzzles. Bull's-eye!
jokes.setup: Engineer suggesting video games for kids in back seat
jokes.punchline: Homer fires him and approves child restraint dome instead
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from reasonable to dystopian child control solutions

jokes.text: And when I gun the motor...I want people to think the world is coming to an end!
jokes.setup: Homer describing his ideal car engine
jokes.punchline: Apocalyptic sound requirements for personal vehicle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer excess, wanting maximum dramatic impact from mundane car feature

jokes.text: To think I wasted my life in boardrooms and meetings. I could have been watching cartoons!
jokes.setup: Herb watching Itchy & Scratchy with kids
jokes.punchline: Businessman realizing cartoons are better than business
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Herb's newfound family perspective

jokes.text: This old fool's wasted his life.
jokes.setup: Watching family interact with Herb's wealth
jokes.punchline: Grampa's self-deprecating realization
jokes.explanation: Typical Grampa self-pity, economical but predictable

jokes.text: All my life, I've looked for a car that feels right. Powerful like a gorilla, yet soft like a Nerf ball.
jokes.setup: Homer describing ideal car qualities
jokes.punchline: Contradictory comparison combining strength and softness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer poetry - contradictory but somehow makes sense

jokes.text: $82,000! This monstrosity costs $82,000?
jokes.setup: Unveiling Homer's designed car
jokes.punchline: Herb's horror at the price of his brother's creation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the car design storyline, Herb's dawning realization of mistake

jokes.text: Maybe I'd have been better off? Maybe? You sponge-head, of course I'd have been better off! As far as I'm concerned, I have no brother!
jokes.setup: Homer's tentative apology about ruining Herb's life
jokes.punchline: Herb's brutal confirmation and family rejection
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation with 'sponge-head' insult and complete family rejection

jokes.text: Maybe he said that to make conversation.
jokes.setup: Herb angrily disowning Homer as brother
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misreading of the emotional devastation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to devastating family rejection, treating it as casual chat

jokes.text: Dad? What is it, boy? I thought your car was cool. Thanks, boy. I was waiting for someone to say that.
jokes.setup: Homer's complete failure and family devastation
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent compliment provides Homer with validation
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending showing child's unconditional support despite adult judgment, perfect father-son moment
